title: Employ Security Engineering Principles
description: Employ security engineering principles in the specification, design, development, implementation, and modification of the system.

checks.name: Security Engineering Principles Policy Exists
checks.purpose: Verify the organization has documented security engineering principles applied to system design and development — policy review only, not automatable

checks.name: Secure Boot Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify Secure Boot is enabled via UEFI, a foundational security engineering control preventing unauthorized boot-time code

checks.name: DEP/NX Memory Protection Enabled
checks.purpose: Confirm Data Execution Prevention is set to OptIn or AlwaysOn via bcdedit to prevent code execution from data pages

checks.name: ASLR Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify Address Space Layout Randomization is enabled in the registry to mitigate memory-based exploits

checks.name: Security Engineering Principles Policy Exists
checks.purpose: Verify documented security engineering principles are applied to server system design — policy review only

checks.name: Secure Boot Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify Secure Boot is enabled on the server, preventing unauthorized firmware and boot-time code execution

checks.name: DEP/NX Memory Protection Enabled
checks.purpose: Confirm DEP/NX is enabled on the server to block exploitation of memory vulnerabilities

checks.name: ASLR Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify ASLR is enabled on the server to randomize memory layout and reduce exploit reliability

checks.name: Security Engineering Principles Policy Exists
checks.purpose: Verify the organization has documented security engineering principles — policy review only, not automatable

checks.name: ASLR Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify kernel.randomize_va_space = 2 to ensure full ASLR is active, randomizing heap, stack, and library addresses

checks.name: SELinux or AppArmor Enforcing
checks.purpose: Confirm mandatory access control is enforcing to implement least-privilege security engineering at the kernel level

checks.name: Secure Boot Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify Secure Boot is active via mokutil to enforce trusted boot chain integrity

checks.name: Security Engineering Principles Policy Exists
checks.purpose: Verify the organization has documented security engineering principles — policy review only, not automatable

checks.name: ASLR Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify kernel.randomize_va_space = 2 to ensure full ASLR is active, randomizing heap, stack, and library addresses

checks.name: SELinux or AppArmor Enforcing
checks.purpose: Confirm mandatory access control is enforcing to implement least-privilege security engineering at the kernel level

checks.name: Secure Boot Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify Secure Boot is active via mokutil to enforce trusted boot chain integrity

remediation: Enable Secure Boot in UEFI firmware settings. Enable DEP via bcdedit /set nx OptIn. Ensure ASLR is active in Windows registry. On Linux, set kernel.randomize_va_space=2 in /etc/sysctl.conf, enable AppArmor or SELinux in enforcing mode, and add fips=1 to GRUB kernel parameters if required.
